title: Pilot Playwright trace-on-retry across tenant smoke suites
rationale: The Playwright 1.53 trace-on-retry improvement makes flaky-run debugging cheaper; piloting it strengthens our smoke-test evidence.

title: Add a human-review gate for AI-assisted test generation
rationale: AI-assisted testing matured this month; a review gate protects against weak assertions and false confidence in our QA pipeline.

title: Evaluate Trace Viewer in the ticket evidence workflow
rationale: Interactive traces could improve the evidence we attach to FHS tickets; worth a small evaluation before adopting broadly.

title: Adopt structured LLM output for all test-case generation
rationale: TW Radar Vol 34 promotes Structured Output from LLMs to Adopt. Our test-case generation pipeline currently uses free-text LLM output; moving to structured JSON via Instructor/Pydantic AI improves reliability and enables automatic validation.

title: Wire feedback sensors into the QA agentic workflow
rationale: TW Radar promotes feedback sensors (linters, compilers, test suites) as gates in agentic workflows. Our validate:release chain (Zod + salience + format) is already a candidate sensor — wire it into agent sessions so failures trigger self-correction before commit.

title: Track DORA rework-rate metric for AI-generated test quality
rationale: DORA now includes rework rate as 5th metric, specifically relevant to AI-assisted development where generated code quality must be tracked. Adding it to our QA metrics gives early warning on AI-generated test degradation.
